# SMDT — Social Media Data Toolkit
> Ingest, standardize, pseudonymize, enrich, and analyze social media data across 11+ platforms.

## Quick Links
- Home: https://varollab.com/SMDT/
- Install: https://varollab.com/SMDT/installation/
- API Reference: https://varollab.com/SMDT/api/

## Core Concepts
- SMDT normalizes heterogeneous social media exports (Twitter/X, Bluesky, TruthSocial, Reddit, Telegram, Gab, Koo, Parler, Voat, Weibo) into one relational Postgres schema.
- Platforms: 11+ supported via 15+ standardizer variants.
- Schema: 5 core tables (Communities, Accounts, Posts, Actions, Entities) + 2 enrichment tables.
- Database: Postgres + TimescaleDB + PostGIS. One database per dataset.
- Pipeline: discover files → plan → standardize → dedup → bulk insert with 3-tier fallback (COPY → VALUES → SAVEPOINT).
- Pseudonymization: DB-to-DB ETL with configurable column policy (hash/redact/drop/blank/keep), regex redactor, optional Presidio-based PII detection, GDPR erasure.
- Enrichment: bot detection, toxicity, language detection, LLM labeling, embeddings. Privacy preprocessor redacts before external API calls.
- Networks: user interaction, entity co-occurrence, bipartite, coaction graphs. Temporal window support.
- Cross-platform: MultiStore attaches multiple Postgres DBs into one DuckDB connection for SQL joins across datasets.
- Erasure: forward-only GDPR right-to-erasure. No reverse mapping stored.
